docket_entries.description: CRC 3.740 REVIEW. (610)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION FOR ORDER ENTERING JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFT PER CCP 664.6 - THE MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE A COURTESY COPY OF THE PAPERS AS REQUIRED BY SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE COURT ALSO DID NOT RECEIVE A PROOF OF SERVICE. JUDGE: MARLA J. LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S APPLICATION FOR ORDER ENTERING JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6 IS GRANTED IN PART IN THE AMOUNT OF $13,627.73. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE MOVING PARTY HAS NOT ESTABLISHED IT IS ENTITLED TO $474.50 IN "COURT COSTS." THE STIPULATION FOR CONDITIONAL ENTRY OF JUDGMENT DOES NOT APPEAR TO REFERENCE THIS ITEM, NOR DOES THE DECLARATION OF LISA D. DUBOWKSI. (CF. DUBOWSKI DECLARATION PARA. 5; "DEFENDANT AGREED TO PAY PLAINTIFF $18,127.73, INCLUSIVE OF COURT COSTS.") ORDER SIGNED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE JOHN K. STEWART; Clerk EBura; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Continued To Aug-04-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Application And Application For Order Entering Judgment Against Deft Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar Apr-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S Application For Order Entering Judgment Against Defendant Pursuant To CCP 664.6 is Granted in part in the amount of $13,627.73. No opposition filed. The moving party has not established it is entitled to $474.50 in "Court Costs." The Stipulation for Conditional Entry of Judgment does not appear to reference this item, nor does the Declaration of Lisa D. Dubowksi. (Cf. Dubowski Declaration para. 5; "Defendant agreed to pay Plaintiff $18,127.73, inclusive of court costs.") ORDER SIGNED
WITH THE MOVING PAPERS. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Notice Of Application For Order Entering Judgment Against Deft Per Ccp 664.6
Off Calendar Mar-07-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK'S Notice Of Application For Order Entering Judgment Against Deft Per Ccp 664.6 - The motion is off calendar. The Court did not receive a courtesy copy of the papers as required by San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The Court also did not receive a proof of service. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
